    <h1 {...{
      "id": "debugging-techniques"
    }}>{`Debugging Techniques`}</h1>
    <p>{`Use console logs and dialog methods to debug your plugin quickly`}</p>
    <p>{`While the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/tutorials/udt-deep-dive/index.md"
      }}>{`UXP Developer Tool`}</a>{` offers a full debugging experience with breakpoints and the Chrome DevTools, these simple techniques can help you quickly troubleshoot issues during development.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{

    <p>{`Before you begin, make sure your development environment uses the following versions:`}</p>

      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong>{` or higher`}</li>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "console-logging"
    }}>{`Console Logging`}</h2>
    <p>{`The simplest way to debug is to log values to the console. Open the UXP Developer Tool console to view output.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Basic logging
console.log("Plugin initialized");          // 💡 General information
console.warn("This feature is deprecated"); // ⚠️ Warnings (yellow)
console.error("Failed to load data");       // ❌ Errors (red)

// Log variables and objects
const user = { name: "Jane", role: "Editor" };
console.log("User data:", user);
// Logs: User data: { name: "Jane", role: "Editor" }

// Log multiple values using template literals
const width = 1920;
const height = 1080;
console.log(\`Resolution: \${width}x\${height}\`); // Template literals for formatting
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "dialog-based-debugging"
    }}>{`Dialog-based Debugging`}</h2>
    <p>{`For quick checks without switching to the console, use dialog methods to display information or input values directly in the UI.`}</p>

    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`alert()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`confirm()`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`prompt()`}</inlineCode>{` methods are not fully supported in Premiere; they will be fixed in a future release.`}</p>

      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`prompt()`}</inlineCode></a>{`, you must enable the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`enableAlerts`}</inlineCode>{` feature flag in your `}<a parentName="p" {...{

// Confirmation dialog
const confirmed = confirm("Do you want to continue?");
if (confirmed) {
  console.log("User clicked OK");
} else {
  console.log("User clicked Cancel");
}

// Prompt dialog for user input

console.log(\`User entered: \${userName}\`);
`}</code></pre>
